materials: Abaca rope, bone, cotton cord, and tortoise shell
description: This stake cord, used for tying up a fighting cock, consists of braided abaca rope. An elongated, triangle-shaped white animal bone is knotted onto one end to lock it. On the other end, a braided blue and yellow cotton cord is tied and looped, serving as an adjustable lock. At the center, a two-holed, oval carved cord organizer made of tortoise shell connects the two separate braided abaca cords.
condition: Fair and stable condition, with a few abrasions on the bone and a few loose threads.

images.alt: Cockfighting tether of braided abaca rope coiled into a loop, a carved bone lock at one end and a knotted blue-and-yellow cord organizer at the other
The chicken stake's bone toggle and abaca cord seen from another angle, showing the tortoiseshell cord organizer where the two braided lengths meet

rights: Images courtesy of the Ethnologisches Museum, Staatliche Museen zu Berlin.
